revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kamifunazuka Kofun | native_name = 上船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|12.79|N|135|48|50.81|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:KamiFunazuka Kofun.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Kamifunazuka Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in what is now part of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]].The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;" |+ Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster |- ! name !! Type !! Length !! date |- | [[Jōnozuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 100m || early 5th century |- | Shiroyama Kofun || keyhole || 63m || mid 5th century |- | [[Nishizuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 74m || end 5th century |- | [[Nakatsuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 72m || late 5th century |- | Jūsen-no-mori Kofun || keyhole || 68m || early 6th century |- | Kamifunazuka Kofun || keyhole || 70m || early 6th century |- | [[Shimofunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 85m || mid 6th century |}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kamifunazuka Kofun | native_name = 上船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|12.79|N|135|48|50.81|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:KamiFunazuka Kofun.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Kamifunazuka Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in what is now part of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]].The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Length !! date |- | [[Jōnozuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 100m || early 5th century |- | Shiroyama Kofun || keyhole || 63m || mid 5th century |- | [[Nishizuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 74m || end 5th century |- | [[Nakatsuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 72m || late 5th century |- | Jūsen-no-mori Kofun || keyhole || 68m || early 6th century |- | Kamifunazuka Kofun || keyhole || 70m || early 6th century |- | [[Shimofunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 85m || mid 6th century |}

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kamifunazuka Kofun | native_name = 上船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|12.79|N|135|48|50.81|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:KamiFunazuka Kofun.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Kamifunazuka Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in what is now part of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]].The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kamifunazuka Kofun | native_name = 上船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|12.79|N|135|48|50.81|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:KamiFunazuka Kofun.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Kamifunazuka Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]].The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]].The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between [[:en:Japan National Route 27|Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]].The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from [[:en:Shin-Hirano Station|Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. ==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between [[:en:Japan National Route 27|Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]].The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. ==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between [[:en:Japan National Route 27|Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]].The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from [[:en:Shin-Hirano Station|Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between [[:en:Japan National Route 27|Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]].The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from [[:en:Shin-Hirano Station|Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[en:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[ja:上船塚古墳]][[zh:上船塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17990214]] Wikidata name: Kamifunazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17990214]] Wikidata name: Kamifunazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kamifunazuka Kofun | native_name = 上船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:KamiFunazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.470194|N|135.81425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between [[:en:Japan National Route 27|Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]].The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from [[:en:Shin-Hirano Station|Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[en:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[ja:上船塚古墳]][[zh:上船塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17990214]] Wikidata name: Kamifunazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17990214]] Wikidata name: Kamifunazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kamifunazuka Kofun | native_name = 上船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:KamiFunazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.470194|N|135.81425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between [[:en:Japan National Route 27|Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]].The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from [[:en:Shin-Hirano Station|Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Length !! date |- | [[Jōnozuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 100m || early 5th century |- | Shiroyama Kofun || keyhole || 63m || mid 5th century |- | [[Nishizuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 74m || end 5th century |- | [[Nakatsuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 72m || late 5th century |- | Jūsen-no-mori Kofun || keyhole || 68m || early 6th century |- | Kamifunazuka Kofun || keyhole || 70m || early 6th century |- | [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 85m || mid 6th century |}

Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.470194|N|135.81425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between [[:en:Japan National Route 27|Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]].The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from [[:en:Shin-Hirano Station|Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;" |+ Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster |- ! name !! type !! Length !! date |- | [[Jōnozuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 100m || early 5th century |- | Shiroyama Kofun || keyhole || 63m || mid 5th century |- | [[Nishizuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 74m || end 5th century |- | [[Nakatsuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 72m || late 5th century |- | Jūsen-no-mori Kofun || keyhole || 68m || early 6th century |- | Kamifunazuka Kofun || keyhole || 70m || early 6th century |- | [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 85m || mid 6th century |}

Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.470194|N|135.81425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between [[:en:Japan National Route 27|Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]].The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from [[:en:Shin-Hirano Station|Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Length !! date |- | [[Jōnozuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 100m || early 5th century |- | Shiroyama Kofun || keyhole || 63m || mid 5th century |- | [[Nishizuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 74m || end 5th century |- | [[Nakatsuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 72m || late 5th century |- | Jūsen-no-mori Kofun || keyhole || 68m || early 6th century |- | Kamifunazuka Kofun || keyhole || 70m || early 6th century |- | [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 85m || mid 6th century |}

Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.470194|N|135.81425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between [[:en:Japan National Route 27|Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]].The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from [[:en:Shin-Hirano Station|Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Length !! date |- | [[Jōnozuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 100m || early 5th century |- | Shiroyama Kofun || keyhole || 63m || mid 5th century |- | [[Nishizuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 74m || end 5th century |- | [[Nakatsuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 72m || late 5th century |- | Jūsen-no-mori Kofun || keyhole || 68m || early 6th century |- | Kamifunazuka Kofun || keyhole || 70m || early 6th century |- | [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 85m || mid 6th century |}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[en:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[ja:上船塚古墳]][[zh:上船塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[en:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[ja:上船塚古墳]][[zh:上船塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[en:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[ja:上船塚古墳]][[zh:上船塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[en:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[ja:上船塚古墳]][[zh:上船塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17990214]] Wikidata name: Kamifunazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17990214]] Wikidata name: Kamifunazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kamifunazuka Kofun | native_name = 上船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:KamiFunazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.470194|N|135.81425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between {{ill|Japan National Route 27|en|Japan National Route 27|WD=Q5356940|lt=Japan National Route 27}} and the tracks of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line}} railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato}}.The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=imperial dynasty}} control over the area of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa}} was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from {{ill|Shin-Hirano Station|en|Shin-Hirano Station|WD=Q7497091|lt=Shin-Hirano Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;" |+ Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster |- ! name !! type !! Length !! date |- | [[Jōnozuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 100m || early 5th century |- | Shiroyama Kofun || keyhole || 63m || mid 5th century |- | [[Nishizuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 74m || end 5th century |- | [[Nakatsuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 72m || late 5th century |- | Jūsen-no-mori Kofun || keyhole || 68m || early 6th century |- | Kamifunazuka Kofun || keyhole || 70m || early 6th century |- | [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 85m || mid 6th century |}
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|WD=Q10222682|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)}} *[[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:}} *[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/japan_heritage/bunkazai/detail.php?lang=EN Obama city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/wakasacho-kamifunazuka.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[en:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[ja:上船塚古墳]][[zh:上船塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[en:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[ja:上船塚古墳]][[zh:上船塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[en:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[ja:上船塚古墳]][[zh:上船塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[en:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[ja:上船塚古墳]][[zh:上船塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17990214]] Wikidata name: Kamifunazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17990214]] Wikidata name: Kamifunazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kamifunazuka Kofun | native_name = 上船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:KamiFunazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.470194|N|135.81425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between {{ill|Japan National Route 27|en|Japan National Route 27|WD=Q5356940|lt=Japan National Route 27}} and the tracks of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line}} railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato}}.The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=imperial dynasty}} control over the area of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa}} was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from {{ill|Shin-Hirano Station|en|Shin-Hirano Station|WD=Q7497091|lt=Shin-Hirano Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=imperial dynasty}} control over the area of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa}} was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from {{ill|Shin-Hirano Station|en|Shin-Hirano Station|WD=Q7497091|lt=Shin-Hirano Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kamifunazuka Kofun | native_name = 上船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:KamiFunazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.470194|N|135.81425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between {{ill|Japan National Route 27|en|Japan National Route 27|WD=Q5356940|lt=Japan National Route 27}} and the tracks of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line}} railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato}}.The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=imperial dynasty}} control over the area of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa}} was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from {{ill|Shin-Hirano Station|en|Shin-Hirano Station|WD=Q7497091|lt=Shin-Hirano Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato}}.The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=imperial dynasty}} control over the area of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa}} was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from {{ill|Shin-Hirano Station|en|Shin-Hirano Station|WD=Q7497091|lt=Shin-Hirano Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
