The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui|1=Wakasa, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between {{ill|Japan National Route 27|en|Japan National Route 27|WD=Q5356940|lt=Japan National Route 27|1=Japan National Route 27}} and the tracks of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}} railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}.The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=imperial dynasty|1=Imperial House of Japan}} control over the area of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa|1=Wakasa Province}} was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from {{ill|Shin-Hirano Station|en|Shin-Hirano Station|WD=Q7497091|lt=Shin-Hirano Station|1=Shin-Hirano Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui|1=Wakasa, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between {{ill|Japan National Route 27|en|Japan National Route 27|WD=Q5356940|lt=Japan National Route 27|1=Japan National Route 27}} and the tracks of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}} railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}.The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=imperial dynasty|1=Imperial House of Japan}} control over the area of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa|1=Wakasa Province}} was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from {{ill|Shin-Hirano Station|en|Shin-Hirano Station|WD=Q7497091|lt=Shin-Hirano Station|1=Shin-Hirano Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[en:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[ja:上船塚古墳]][[zh:上船塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[en:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[ja:上船塚古墳]][[zh:上船塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[en:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[ja:上船塚古墳]][[zh:上船塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[en:Kamifunazuka Kofun]][[ja:上船塚古墳]][[zh:上船塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kamifunazuka Kofun | native_name = 上船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:KamiFunazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.470194|N|135.81425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui|1=Wakasa, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui|1=Wakasa, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between {{ill|Japan National Route 27|en|Japan National Route 27|WD=Q5356940|lt=Japan National Route 27|1=Japan National Route 27}} and the tracks of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}} railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}.The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=imperial dynasty|1=Imperial House of Japan}} control over the area of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa|1=Wakasa Province}} was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from {{ill|Shin-Hirano Station|en|Shin-Hirano Station|WD=Q7497091|lt=Shin-Hirano Station|1=Shin-Hirano Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Length !! date |- | [[Jōnozuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 100m || early 5th century |- | Shiroyama Kofun || keyhole || 63m || mid 5th century |- | [[Nishizuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 74m || end 5th century |- | [[Nakatsuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 72m || late 5th century |- | Jūsen-no-mori Kofun || keyhole || 68m || early 6th century |- | Kamifunazuka Kofun || keyhole || 70m || early 6th century |- | [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 85m || mid 6th century |}

The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=imperial dynasty|1=Imperial House of Japan}} control over the area of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|qid=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa|1=Wakasa Province}} was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|qid=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from {{ill|Shin-Hirano Station|en|Shin-Hirano Station|qid=Q7497091|lt=Shin-Hirano Station|1=Shin-Hirano Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|qid=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kamifunazuka Kofun'''|上船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|qid=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui|1=Wakasa, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216369 |title= 上船塚古墳 |trans-title= Kamifunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamifunazuka Kofun is one of a group of ''kofun'' located in the Hirano neighbourhood of central Wakasa town, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is sandwiched between {{ill|Japan National Route 27|en|Japan National Route 27|qid=Q5356940|lt=Japan National Route 27|1=Japan National Route 27}} and the tracks of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|qid=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}} railway. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east and is separated from the [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected this region with {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}.The total length of the tumulus is 70 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and height of 16 meters. The "neck" of the structure has a width of 20 meters, widening out towards the west, where the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 50 meters. The height of the tumulus is 16 meters. The entire structure is similar to that of the nearby [[Nishizuka Kofun]]. The Kamifunazuka Kofun was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'', of which a portion remain, and the fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the area. The ''kofun'' is also surrounded by a dry moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamifunazuka Kofun is believed to date from the early 6th century AD, or late in the [[Kofun period]], but this tumulus has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by archaeologists. It may have been the grave a local king before {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=imperial dynasty|1=Imperial House of Japan}} control over the area of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|qid=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa|1=Wakasa Province}} was firmly established. Per local legend, this tumulus is said to be the grave of one of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|qid=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 13-minute walk from {{ill|Shin-Hirano Station|en|Shin-Hirano Station|qid=Q7497091|lt=Shin-Hirano Station|1=Shin-Hirano Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|qid=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
